definitions.text: To habitually go to (someone in distress, sickness etc.) to comfort them. (Now generally merged into later senses, below.) .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
To go and meet (a person) as an act of friendliness or sociability.
examples: She decided to visit her grandparents for Christmas.

definitions.text: Of God: to appear to (someone) to comfort, bless, or chastise or punish them. (Now generally merged into later senses, below.)

examples: Next time you're in Manchester, give me a visit.
We paid a quick visit to James on the way up to Scotland.

definitions.text: A meeting with a doctor at their surgery or the doctor's at one's home.
